faculty.name: Paul Withers
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Upper atmospheres and ionospheres of planets, especially Mars. Radio occultation and accelerometer measurements from spacecraft.

faculty.name: Catherine Espaillat
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair; Director, Institute for Astrophysical Research
faculty.summary: Structure and evolution of circumstellar disks around young stars. Planet formation, dust growth, radiative transfer modeling. Observations with JWST, ALMA.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Blanton
faculty.title: Professor; Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Galaxy clusters, radio galaxies, and high-energy astrophysics. Multiwavelength studies of clusters and AGN feedback.

faculty.name: Tereasa Brainerd
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmology. Dark matter halos of galaxies, weak gravitational lensing, large-scale structure, and galaxy evolution.

faculty.name: Michael Mendillo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Space physics and planetary atmospheres. Ionospheric observations and models, comparative planetary atmospheres.

faculty.name: Merav Opher
faculty.title: Professor; Director, SHIELD NASA Drive Center
faculty.summary: Computational/theoretical plasma physics. Heliosphere structure, solar wind interaction with the interstellar medium, and astrospheres. Leads NASA SHIELD DRIVE Science Center.

faculty.name: Meers Oppenheim
faculty.title: Professor; Associate Director, Center for Space Physics
faculty.summary: Space plasma physics. E-region ionosphere and electrojet dynamics, meteor trail plasma, and wave-particle interactions in space plasmas.

faculty.name: Thomas Bania
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Radio astronomy and spectroscopy. Galactic structure, interstellar medium, and the Galactic Center. Studies of helium abundance in the Galaxy.

faculty.name: Alan Marscher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Active galactic nuclei, quasars, and blazars. Relativistic jets from supermassive black holes. Multiwavelength observations from radio to gamma rays.

faculty.name: JJ Hermes
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Stellar astrophysics of white dwarf stars. Pulsating white dwarfs, planetary debris around white dwarfs, compact binary evolution.

faculty.name: Wen Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Magnetospheric physics. Aurora, radiation belt dynamics, and wave-particle interactions in Earth's magnetosphere.

faculty.name: Philip Muirhead
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director, Perkins Telescope Observatory
faculty.summary: Exoplanets and low-mass stars. Occurrence and characterization of planets around M dwarfs. Astronomical instrumentation. Director of Perkins Telescope Observatory.

faculty.name: Toshi Nishimura
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Admissions
faculty.summary: Magnetosphere-ionosphere coupling. Aurora dynamics, space weather, and ionospheric disturbances. Multisatellite and ground-based observations.

faculty.name: Dillon Brout
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; Director of Graduate Admissions
faculty.summary: Supernova cosmology. Dark energy, Hubble constant tension. Leads SH0ES, Pantheon+, DES supernova cosmology, and DESI analyses.

faculty.name: Emily Cunningham
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Dark matter in the Milky Way. Using stellar kinematics and galactic archaeology to constrain the Milky Way's dark matter distribution.

faculty.name: Chuanfei Dong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Computational space plasma physics. Solar wind interaction with planets, Mars atmospheric escape, magnetospheric physics, and exoplanetary space environments.

programs.program_name: Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences PhD

requirements.admission_model: Advisor-driven; contacting faculty before applying is encouraged

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition + stipend ($50,000/yr from Sept 2026)

Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Jonathan Lunine
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy (Chair)
faculty.summary: Department chair and planetary scientist studying giant planets, planetary atmospheres, astrobiology, and the search for habitable worlds including Titan and ocean worlds.

faculty.name: Rachel Bean
faculty.title: Jacob Gould Schurman Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Cosmologist testing theories of dark energy and modified gravity using cosmic microwave background, large-scale structure, and weak gravitational lensing.

faculty.name: Nicholas Battaglia
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Cosmologist using the Sunyaev-Zeldovich effect and CMB data to study galaxy clusters, cosmic structure formation, and the thermal history of the universe.

faculty.name: Shami Chatterjee
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Radio astronomer studying pulsars and neutron stars, using them as probes of fundamental physics, gravitational waves, and the interstellar medium.

faculty.name: David F. Chernoff
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy (Director of Graduate Studies)
faculty.summary: Director of graduate studies and theoretical astrophysicist studying cosmic strings, gravitational lensing, and dynamical evolution of stellar systems.

faculty.name: James M. Cordes
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Radio astronomer and NANOGrav collaborator studying pulsars as probes of the interstellar medium, gravitational wave backgrounds, and fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Alexander G. Hayes
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Planetary scientist studying the surfaces and atmospheres of outer solar system bodies, particularly Titan, using Cassini and remote sensing data.

faculty.name: Martha P. Haynes
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Arts and Sciences in Astronomy
faculty.summary: Extragalactic astronomer leading the ALFALFA survey, using HI 21cm observations to study galaxy evolution, gas content, and large-scale structure.

faculty.name: Terry L. Herter
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Infrared astronomer developing instrumentation for ground-based and space-based observatories to study star formation and the galactic center.

faculty.name: Lisa Kaltenegger
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy; Director of the Carl Sagan Institute
faculty.summary: Director of the Carl Sagan Institute studying exoplanet atmospheres, biosignatures, and the characterization of potentially habitable worlds using JWST and future missions.

faculty.name: Dong Lai
faculty.title: Professor of Astrophysics
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist studying the formation and evolution of binary systems, including black hole and neutron star mergers, and exoplanetary system dynamics.

faculty.name: Ness Lewis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Observational astronomer characterizing exoplanet atmospheres using transmission and emission spectroscopy with ground-based telescopes and JWST.

faculty.name: Philip D. Nicholson
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Planetary scientist studying planetary ring systems, orbital dynamics, and using stellar occultations to probe planetary atmospheres.

faculty.name: Michael Niemack
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Experimental cosmologist developing CMB instrumentation for the Atacama Cosmology Telescope and Simons Observatory to probe inflationary physics.

faculty.name: Dominik A. Riechers
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Extragalactic astronomer using ALMA and other submillimeter facilities to study the interstellar medium and star formation in high-redshift galaxies.

faculty.name: Britney Schmidt
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.sub_field: Planetary Science / Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Planetary scientist studying icy moons and ocean worlds including Europa, using geophysical methods and developing robotic exploration technologies.

faculty.name: Gordon J. Stacey
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy (Director of Undergraduate Studies)
faculty.summary: Infrared/submillimeter astronomer studying the interstellar medium and star formation in galaxies using far-infrared spectroscopy and instrumentation.

faculty.name: Ira M. Wasserman
faculty.title: Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist working on neutron star physics, cosmology, dark energy models, cosmic strings, and general relativistic astrophysics.

faculty.name: N. Peter Armitage
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist investigating exotic electronic states at low temperatures using terahertz and microwave spectroscopy, including superconductors, quantum spin liquids, topological insulators, and Weyl/Dirac semimetals.

faculty.name: Collin Broholm
faculty.title: Gerhard H. Dieke Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Institute for Quantum Matter (IQM). Experimentalist using neutron scattering to study quantum disordered phases, frustrated magnetism in 2D and 3D, and strongly correlated magnetic superconductors.

faculty.name: Tyrel M. McQueen
faculty.title: Professor and Henry A. Rowland Chair
faculty.summary: Solid state chemist and condensed matter physicist focused on discovering new materials with exotic electronic states. Directs the NSF-funded PARADIM Bulk Crystal Growth Facility for quantum materials synthesis.

faculty.name: Robert L. Leheny
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair and experimental soft condensed matter physicist studying complex fluids, glass-forming liquids, liquid crystal-colloid composites, gels, and biofilms using X-ray Photon Correlation Spectroscopy (XPCS). Elected APS Fellow in 2024.

faculty.name: Daniel H. Reich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist spanning hard condensed matter (quantum magnets, superconductor-ferromagnet heterostructures) and soft matter/biological physics (cell mechanics, active matter microrheology).

faculty.name: Yi Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist exploring exotic quantum phases including topological superconductivity, itinerant ferromagnetism, and flat band physics using non-perturbative analytic and quantum Monte Carlo methods. Sloan Fellow (2018).

faculty.name: Matthieu Wyart
faculty.title: W. H. Miller Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist studying the glass transition, yielding transition, jamming of granular materials, marginal stability in amorphous solids, and the theory of deep learning. Simons Investigator. Joined JHU in November 2024 from EPFL.

faculty.name: Charles L. Bennett
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist and Principal Investigator of NASA's WMAP mission, which established the Standard Cosmological Model. Co-PI of the CLASS telescope mapping CMB polarization from Chile to search for inflationary gravitational waves. Recipient of the Breakthrough Prize (2018).

faculty.name: Adam G. Riess
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Nobel Laureate in Physics (2011) for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe. Leads the SH0ES program using Hubble and JWST to precisely measure the Hubble constant and investigate the Hubble tension suggesting possible new physics.

faculty.name: Marc Kamionkowski
faculty.title: William R. Kenan, Jr. Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical cosmologist whose work spans dark matter, cosmic microwave background, inflation, dark energy, and gravitational waves. Pioneered tools for CMB analysis and early-universe probes. Member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

faculty.name: Timothy M. Heckman
faculty.title: Dr. A. Hermann Pfund Professor
faculty.summary: Observational astrophysicist studying galaxy formation and evolution, the co-evolution of galaxies and supermassive black holes, and galactic superwinds. Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Former Department Chair (2015-2021).

faculty.name: Rosemary F. G. Wyse
faculty.title: Alumni Centennial Professor
faculty.summary: World-leading expert in galactic archaeology, using the fossil record in old stars to decode the formation history of the Milky Way. Studies stellar populations, dark matter distribution, and galaxy formation physics.

faculty.name: Nadia L. Zakamska
faculty.title: Professor and Vice Chair for Academics
faculty.summary: Observational and theoretical astrophysicist studying quasar-driven winds and their role in galaxy evolution, dual supermassive black holes in mergers, and compact object binaries as gravitational wave progenitors. Recipient of the AAS Newton Lacy Pierce Prize.

faculty.name: Julian H. Krolik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist specializing in black hole accretion, active galactic nuclei, relativistic MHD simulations, and electromagnetic counterparts of gravitational wave sources. Author of the standard graduate text Active Galactic Nuclei.

faculty.name: Joseph Silk
faculty.title: Homewood Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Pioneering theoretical cosmologist whose work on CMB damping (Silk damping) and galaxy formation shaped modern cosmology. Recipient of the Balzan Prize (2011) and Henry Norris Russell Lectureship (2018).

faculty.name: Tobias Marriage
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist co-leading the CLASS CMB polarization telescope in Chile, searching for inflationary B-mode signatures. Also studies galaxy clusters via the Sunyaev-Zeldovich effect and serves as Director of Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: Alessandra Corsi
faculty.title: W. H. Miller Professor
faculty.summary: Multi-messenger time-domain astronomer studying relativistic transients and gravitational wave sources via radio follow-up. Key leadership roles in LIGO, Cosmic Explorer, and the JHU-NRAO ngVLA partnership. Recipient of the 2022 New Horizons in Physics Prize.

faculty.name: David K. Sing
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Exoplanetary physicist leading the largest Hubble exoplanet program (Panchromatic Comparative Exoplanetary Treasury Survey), characterizing exoplanet atmospheres. Uses JWST and HST transmission spectroscopy to measure atmospheric composition, clouds, and core masses.

faculty.name: Kevin Schlaufman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Observational astrophysicist at the intersection of exoplanets and galactic astronomy, studying planet formation and evolution. Leads the first all-sky search for the oldest and most chemically primitive stars in the Milky Way.

faculty.name: Meredith MacGregor
faculty.title: Vera C. Rubin Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Observational astrophysicist using multi-wavelength facilities (ALMA, JWST, HST) to study planet formation in debris disks, exoplanet habitability, and the impact of stellar flares on planetary atmospheres. Deputy PI of FIRSST space telescope concept.

faculty.name: Benjamin D. Wandelt
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Cosmologist and data scientist bridging cosmology with AI/ML, developing Bayesian inference methods (BORG algorithm, Gibbs sampling for CMB) to extract fundamental physics from astronomical surveys. Co-recipient of the 2018 Gruber Cosmology Prize with Planck.

faculty.name: David E. Kaplan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist working on extensions to the Standard Model, dark matter theory, novel experimental approaches, and the hierarchy problem. Creator and producer of the documentary film Particle Fever about the LHC. Simons Investigator.

faculty.name: Andrei Gritsan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high-energy physicist on the CMS experiment at the LHC, contributing to the Higgs boson discovery and characterization. Developer of the MELA technique and JHU Generator framework for measuring spin/parity of new resonances at hadron colliders.

faculty.name: Surjeet Rajendran
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist inventing novel experimental methods to detect dark matter (axion/ultralight), dark energy, and high-frequency gravitational waves using NMR, semiconductor detectors, and quantum sensing techniques.

faculty.name: Ibrahima Bah
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical high-energy physicist working on string theory, supergravity, holography, and geometric engineering of QFTs. Studies topological solitons in gravity and black hole microstate geometries. Former UC President's Postdoctoral Fellow.

faculty.name: David DeMille
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: World-leading AMO physicist specializing in precision measurement using cold and ultracold polar molecules to test fundamental symmetries and search for the electron electric dipole moment. Elected to the National Academy of Sciences (2024). Ramsey Prize recipient.

faculty.name: Christopher Overstreet
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist using ytterbium atom interferometry to perform precision tests of fundamental physics, including searches for ultralight dark matter and tests of the equivalence principle.
